title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: RevOps role partnering with Sales leadership, Strategy, and Finance to optimize territory composition and coverage plans. Requires deep understanding of B2B SaaS go-to-market models, sales territories, and segmentation strategies, plus strong analytical skills in Excel, SQL, and BI tools (Tableau, Retool). Proficiency with Salesforce required; experience with territory management tools like Fullcast, Pigment, or Anaplan a plus. Involves redesigning territory models for global scalability, maintaining CRM data integrity, and supporting GTM planning cycles. Salary range $149,000–$175,000.
note: Posted April 2026 — may be older than 30 days; verify still open.
score_reason: Exceptional match for Omri's RevOps expertise and B2B SaaS background. Territory strategy, Salesforce administration, pipeline forecasting, and analytical rigor align perfectly with role requirements; remote US location is acceptable.
ai_opener: Omri here—15+ years optimizing B2B SaaS go-to-market operations. I've spent the last decade designing territory models, forecasting pipelines, and scaling RevOps functions for high-growth teams. Your Territory Strategy & Operations role at Gong caught my eye because territory design is where RevOps actually drives revenue—and I've seen firsthand how the right segmentation model compounds growth.

title: RevOps Expert
description: Joins the Revenue Operations team to optimize sales processes and system architecture. Drives strategic initiatives aligned with GTM strategy, champions scalable process design, and implements solutions using Six Sigma and Agile methodologies. Requires extensive Salesforce (SFDC) and enterprise SaaS tool expertise, root-cause analysis skills, and experience with automation tools like LeanData, Zapier, Camunda. Proven SaaS/B2B and remote/global startup experience preferred. Focus on system integration, data integrity, automation, and documentation.
score_reason: Excellent match for a senior RevOps executive—remote global role, strategic GTM focus, Salesforce expertise, and B2B SaaS background align perfectly with Omri's core strengths. The emphasis on process optimization, automation tools, and system architecture directly leverages his 15+ years of revenue operations leadership.

Not a CRM admin role — writes code and architects net-new systems.
